summary: Tail-recursive Euclidean GCD on u64 (gcd(a,b) = if b=0 then a else gcd(b, a%b)); closed-form postcondition `= Nat.gcd a.toNat b.toNat` proved by strong induction on the measure b.toNat under partial_fixpoint — base case discharges `Nat.gcd_zero_right`, step case bridges `a%?b` via if_neg + `UInt64.toNat_mod`, applies IH with `Nat.mod_lt`, and threads `Nat.gcd_rec` + `Nat.gcd_comm` (twice) to handle the argument-swap; derived theorems (b=0, a=0, divides_a/b, greatest via `Nat.dvd_gcd`, zero_iff) reuse the closed form as an oracle. Recursive counterpart to gcd_while_modified.
